reasoning: The TGA balance on 2025-10-22 peaked at an exceptionally high $905B due to the mid-October tax receipt surge. Since the Treasury usually targets a cash baseline of around $750B-$850B, the balance normally drops significantly by November after October taxes clear. Therefore, the likelihood of the TGA continuing to increase from this $905B peak in the near term (Nov 02) is quite low. By late November, the regular drawdown cycle traditionally takes TGA to its monthly lows. However, because variance and uncertainty mathematically inherently increase with longer horizons for financial metrics—due to unknown exogenous shocks, shifting Treasury targets, or major subsequent tax seasons/auctions—the probability estimates appropriately drift closer to 0.5 (maximum uncertainty) for dates further out (January 2026).
